name: Slider
summary: Theme-aware draggable slider for selecting a number within `min`/`max` (optionally snapped to `step`); use it for continuous or coarse-grained ranges where the approximate value matters more than an exact typed number.

bestPractices: Use Slider for ranges where relative position matters; prefer `TextField` for precise numeric entry.
Snap deliberately. Provide step increments that match the domain; avoid overly fine steps that make keyboard or touch fiddly.
Label min/max or current value. Show ticks or helper text for context; keep labels short and avoid clutter.
Keyboard support. Ensure Left/Right (or Up/Down) adjust values by one step, and Page/Home/End jump predictably.
Size for touch. Pick token sizes or numeric height to keep the thumb easy to grab on mobile.
Use presets/tokens for colour and shape. Keep theme‑aligned styling rather than per‑instance CSS.

Binding precedence (prop > form > internal). An explicit `value` prop always wins; otherwise a `name` inside a `FormControl` binds to the store (seed the key in `createFormStore(initial)` so the initial value is honoured); otherwise the Slider is uncontrolled. The binding is latched at mount — don't switch a Slider between controlled and uncontrolled.

Change source. `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` report a truthful `info.source`: `'pointer'` for a drag/click on the track and `'keyboard'` for the arrow/Page/Home/End keys, not a blanket `'programmatic'`.

examples.title: Controlled slider
